events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess reversed polarity on receptacles/switches, rough-complete. Reversed polarity is a defect, non-compliant, fail. Need references NEC 406.4 etc. Need fill fields. Let's reason.

Scenario: field service, installed operating asset, electrical, receptacles & switches, observed reversed polarity, rough-complete. Need decide fail. Risk: reversed polarity on receptacles can be shock/equipment hazard, especially if device expects polarized plug. For switches, reversed polarity maybe less critical but still non-compliant. Risk high? Could be high due to shock hazard on receptacles. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Work roughed in not closed out. Value not asked, 0. Sound etc default 0/empty. Findings: reversed polarity at receptacle/switch. Actions: de-energize, correct wiring, verify polarity/continuity, retest. Rationale. References: NEC 406.4 (receptacle replacement/terminal identification/polarity), NEC 406.9 (receptacles in wet/damp locations), NEC 210.8 (GFCI). Reversed polarity violates 406.4? Actually 406.4 covers general installation requirements, replacement, grounding; 406.4(D) maybe terminal identification, grounded conductor connected to identified terminal. Also 200.10? But references on hand only. Use 406.4 and 406.9. 210.8 maybe GFCI not directly reversed polarity. But can mention if receptacle requires GFCI. We'll include all three.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "findings": ["Reversed polarity observed on receptacle/switch wiring"],
  "actions": ["De-energize the circuit", "Remove and re-terminate the device with correct polarity: grounded (neutral) conductor to the identified (silver) terminal and ungrounded (hot) conductor to the brass terminal", "Verify correct wiring with continuity and polarity tests", "Re-energize and confirm proper operation before close-out"],
  "rationale": "Reversed polarity at a receptacle or switch is a wiring defect that violates NEC terminal-identification and installation requirements, creates shock and equipment hazards, and must be corrected before the work can be accepted.",
  "references": ["NEC 210.8", "NEC 406.4", "NEC 406.9"]
}
